name: The Healer's Burden
description: Elara needs rare herbs from Thornwood to brew an antidote for the nightcap poisoning. The forest will not give them freely.

DISCOVERED.journal_entry: Elara mentioned she knows a recipe for an antidote to nightcap poisoning, but the key ingredient — moonpetal — only grows deep in Thornwood.

ACTIVE.journal_entry: I agreed to gather moonpetal from Thornwood for Elara's antidote. She warned me that territorial wolves guard the grove where it grows.
objectives: Travel to the moonpetal grove in Thornwood
Return to Elara with the herbs

HERBS_GATHERED.journal_entry: I have the moonpetal. Elara can now attempt to brew the antidote. She warned me the process is delicate — one wrong measurement and the cure becomes another poison.

ANTIDOTE_BREWED.journal_entry: The antidote is ready. Elara wants to administer it to the three sickest villagers immediately.
objectives: Help Elara administer the antidote to the sick villagers

CHURCH_DISCOVERY.journal_entry: I discovered moonpetal growing in the Church Outpost gardens. The Church had the cure for nightcap poisoning all along — and said nothing.
objectives: Confront Brother Maren about the Church's moonpetal supply
Decide what to do with this information

COMPLETED_SUCCESS.journal_entry: The antidote worked. Three villagers who were on the edge of death are recovering. Elara wept with relief. Ashvale owes her more than it knows.

COMPLETED_FAILURE.journal_entry: The antidote was wrong. Old Thomas died screaming in his bed. Elara won't speak. She just stares at her hands.

COMPLETED_BETRAYAL.journal_entry: I sold the moonpetal to Fenrick. He'll sell it for ten times the price I got. Elara found out. She'll never forgive me.

FAILED_ELARA_DEAD.journal_entry: Elara is gone. The herbs sit in my pack, useless. The antidote she dreamed of will never be brewed. The village's best healer is dead, and the crops keep dying.

COMPLETED_CHURCH_EXPOSED.journal_entry: The village now knows the Church had a cure all along. Brother Maren's influence is cracking. But was it incompetence — or intention?
